title: WELD: The Verifiable Broadcast
tagline: A public feed that can't be silenced or faked: mirrors keep it alive, freshness proofs keep it honest.
holy_shit: Kill the host and the feed keeps broadcasting from a mirror; try to replay a stale value as current and the freshness proof exposes it — censorship-resistant and replay-proof, all in your browser.
powered_by: Kill the Host mirror resolution welded to Proof of Freshness byte-hash timestamps so every broadcast emission is both host-independent and tamper-evident
weld: Each mirror-resolved broadcast value is immediately stamped with a fetch-timestamp and SHA-256 of the exact response bytes, so a silenced host cannot prevent broadcast and a replayed stale value cannot disguise its age.
agent_scenario: When you need a live public feed that survives origin deletion and proves every emission was fetched now, not replayed from a stale snapshot.
author: kody-w + Copilot
